machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin ring (export channel through OM)
machinery_genes.quote: GMI1462, carrying a disruption of the hrcC gene, which encodes the R. solanacearum secretin
machinery_genes.note: Aldon et al 2000 (PrhA plant-contact sensor paper, EMBO J), R. solanacearum GMI1000. hrcC = SctC-family outer-membrane secretin. Independently named as an hrc gene in Van Gijsegem 2002 (PMID 12046592) hrp-cluster dissection.

machinery_genes.quote: for the construction of the hrcJ and hrcU mutants, a fragment internal to the gene was replaced by the apha-3 cassette
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002 genetic dissection of the GMI1000 hrp cluster; hrcJ is one of the conserved hrc genes shared by all type III systems (SctJ inner-ring family). Role from the apparatus brief vocabulary.

machinery_genes.quote: The hrcN, hrcV, hrcR, and hrcS mutations were constructed by cloning the apha-3 cassette directly in a restriction site disrupting the corresponding hrp gene
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; hrcN = SctN-family T3SS ATPase. Named in the mutant-construction methods of the GMI1000 hrp-cluster dissection.

machinery_genes.quote: non-polar mutants in all hrp genes (except for hrcQ) were analysed for their interactions with plants, their ability to secrete the PopA protein and their production of the Hrp pilus
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; hrcQ named as one of the conserved hrc genes of the GMI1000 hrp cluster (it was the only hrp gene not individually mutated in this study). SctQ-family cytoplasmic C-ring role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The hrcN, hrcV, hrcR, and hrcS mutations were constructed by cloning the apha-3 cassette directly in a restriction site disrupting the corresponding hrp gene
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; hrcR = SctR-family export-gate protein of the GMI1000 injectisome basal structure.

machinery_genes.quote: The hrcN, hrcV, hrcR, and hrcS mutations were constructed by cloning the apha-3 cassette directly in a restriction site disrupting the corresponding hrp gene
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; hrcS = SctS-family export-gate protein of the GMI1000 injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: The hrcC and hrcT genes are located at the end of an operon; consequently, mutations already constructed by the insertion of a Tn5-lac transposon were used
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; hrcT = SctT-family export-gate protein, named alongside hrcC in the GMI1000 hrp-cluster mutant set.

machinery_genes.quote: for the construction of the hrcJ and hrcU mutants, a fragment internal to the gene was replaced by the apha-3 cassette
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; hrcU = SctU-family export-gate autoprotease of the GMI1000 injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: The hrcN, hrcV, hrcR, and hrcS mutations were constructed by cloning the apha-3 cassette directly in a restriction site disrupting the corresponding hrp gene
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; hrcV = SctV-family export-apparatus ring. This protein was first sequenced and characterized as HrpO/LcrD-homologue in Gough et al 1993 (PMID 8316211) before the hrc nomenclature; renamed hrcV in the conserved-gene scheme.

machinery_genes.role: ATPase stalk / central stalk of the export apparatus
machinery_genes.quote: non-polar mutants in all hrp genes (except for hrcQ) were analysed for their interactions with plants, their ability to secrete the PopA protein and their production of the Hrp pilus
machinery_genes.note: HrpO is named as a structural component of the GMI1000 hrp cluster. NB: the protein originally called 'HrpO' in Gough et al 1993 (PMID 8316211, MGG) was the LcrD/MxiA homologue (now HrcV, SctV). In the post-1995 hrc nomenclature, hrpO denotes the SctO ATPase-stalk gene retained in the hrp-cluster gene list dissected by Van Gijsegem 2002. Family/role assigned from the apparatus brief (SctO = YscO/HrpO ATPase stalk); lower-confidence on exact SctO assignment due to the historical HrpO/HrcV naming overlap.

machinery_genes.role: major subunit of the Hrp pilus (needle/pilus appendage)
machinery_genes.quote: hrp genes are also involved in the biogenesis of pili that are mainly composed of the HrpY protein
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2000; HrpY is the structural subunit of the Hrp pilus of R. solanacearum GMI1000 (analogous to HrpA pilus / SctF needle in the unified scheme). Also named in Aldon et al 2000: 'The hrpY gene was recently shown to encode a component of an essential extracellular pilus structure of the secretion apparatus' (PMID 10811621).

machinery_genes.role: Hrp pilus assembly factor (required for pilus biogenesis)
machinery_genes.quote: we also provide evidence that hrpV and hrpX mutants can still target the HrpY pilin outside the bacterial cell but are impaired in the production of Hrp pili, indicating that HrpV and HrpX proteins are involved in the assembly of this appendage
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; HrpV is required for Hrp pilus assembly in GMI1000. T3SS-associated structural/assembly component (not a regulator, not an effector). No unified Sct equivalent given; R. solanacearum-specific accessory.

machinery_genes.role: Hrp pilus assembly factor (required for pilus biogenesis)
machinery_genes.quote: we also provide evidence that hrpV and hrpX mutants can still target the HrpY pilin outside the bacterial cell but are impaired in the production of Hrp pili, indicating that HrpV and HrpX proteins are involved in the assembly of this appendage
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; HrpX required for Hrp pilus assembly in GMI1000. NB: this is the R. solanacearum hrp-cluster HrpX assembly protein, NOT the Xanthomonas HrpX transcriptional regulator (different protein, same name). T3SS-associated structural/assembly component.

notes: System: the Hrp (HR and pathogenicity) type III secretion system of Ralstonia solanacearum GMI1000, encoded by a ~23 kb hrp gene cluster on the megaplasmid, organized in seven transcription units totalling ~20 hrp/hrc genes. The injectisome injects the ~70 type III effectors (incl. this instance's RSp-tagged effectors) into plant cells. The cluster was foundationally sequenced and the nine conserved genes renamed hrc (hrp-conserved) by Van Gijsegem et al 1995 (PMID 7623665); systematically mutated gene-by-gene by Van Gijsegem et al 2002 (PMID 12046592), which is the primary apparatus paper here and from whose methods/abstract most naming quotes are drawn. All genes are from GMI1000 itself (no cross-strain transfer needed). Core injectisome covered: secretin (HrcC), basal-body/export-gate ring (HrcJ, HrcR, HrcS, HrcT, HrcU, HrcV), ATPase (HrcN), C-ring (HrcQ), ATPase stalk (HrpO), pilus subunit (HrpY), pilus-assembly accessories (HrpV, HrpX). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the regulatory cascade (PrhA, PrhI/PrhR, PrhJ, HrpG, HrpB, PrhG) and the AraC-family activator HrpB; the secreted harpin/translocators and effectors (PopA/PopB/PopC, PopF1/PopF2, and all Rip/AWR/GALA effectors); and HpaP (hrp-associated, dispensable for plant interactions). LOW-CONFIDENCE CALLS: (1) HrpO SctO-stalk assignment, because the name 'HrpO' historically labelled the LcrD/MxiA homologue (now HrcV) in Gough et al 1993 before the hrc rename, so the modern hrpO=SctO mapping carries some ambiguity; listed because it is named as a structural hrp gene retained in the GMI1000 cluster. (2) HrcQ role assigned from family vocabulary (it was the one gene Van Gijsegem 2002 did not individually mutate). The needle-length ruler (HrpP/SctP), inner rod (HrpB/SctI; note name-clash with the regulator HrpB), gatekeeper (HrpJ/SctW), and the HrpF/PopF translocon were not given individual verbatim naming quotes here and were left off rather than padded, though HrpF/PopF1/PopF2 translocators are documented in Meyer et al 2006 (10.1128/jb.00180-06) if a later pass wants to add them. Apparatus brief Hrc<->Sct mapping used throughout.
